title: Vicarious touch: overlapping neural patterns between seeing and feeling touch
description: This dataset contains whole-brain EEG recordings from participants experiencing tactile stimulation and viewing videos of touch to investigate overlapping neural representations between firsthand and vicarious touch. Using time-resolved multivariate pattern analysis, the study examines whether observed touch evokes similar neural patterns as direct tactile experience. The dataset includes 64-channel EEG data collected at 2048 Hz from 12 experimental runs comprising 1728 trials across tactile and visual conditions.
methods_description: Whole-brain 64-channel EEG data were recorded using an Active Two Biosemi system at 2048 Hz with 10-20 standard electrode placement. Participants completed 12 runs divided into four blocks of 36 trials each, alternating between tactile stimulation to the fingers and viewing matched videos of touch. An 800 ms inter-trial interval separated trials, with each run lasting approximately 7-8 minutes. Stimuli were presented using MATLAB and Psychtoolbox.
